text: The surgeon begins the procedure with a medium approach, identifying and protecting the superior hypogastric plexus. Central lymph nodes around the inferior mesenteric artery (IMA) and vein (IMV) are removed. The left colic artery is isolated and cut at its origin, while the superior rectal artery is retained. Hypogastral nerves are identified and protected. The mesocolon is dissected from median to lateral along the left toes line, with the left ureter identified and protected.

The surgeon ligates and cuts the root of the inferior mesenteric vein (IMV). The mesocolon is dissected further towards the upper border of the pancreas. The sigmoid colon is mobilized laterally, distally towards the rectum, and proximally towards the descending colon. The splenic flexure is mobilized, confirming complete mobilization of the sigmoid and descending colon.
The surgeon removes the specimen through a small incision, followed by extracorporeal anastomosis.
